description: Twitter/X Link → PDF Converter — paste a tweet URL, preview it, download as a clean PDF

userStories.title: Project Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded so I can start building features.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize Bun project with TypeScript (bun init)
Install Hono as the web framework
Install Puppeteer for PDF generation
Create src/index.ts with Hono server that serves static files from public/
Create public/index.html with a basic HTML page
Add dev script in package.json: bun run --watch src/index.ts
Server starts on port 3000 with bun run dev
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: URL Input & Validation UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste a Twitter/X URL so the app knows which tweet to convert.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page has a centered text input with placeholder 'Paste a Twitter/X link...'
Generate PDF button below the input
Client-side validation accepts URLs matching twitter.com/*/status/* and x.com/*/status/*
Shows inline red error text for invalid URLs when user submits
Button is disabled while input is empty
Clean minimal design with CSS (no framework)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented with vanilla JS validation, CSS styling, and proper error states

userStories.title: Fetch Single Tweet via Syndication API
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to fetch tweet content so I can see it before downloading.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/tweet endpoint accepts JSON body with url field
Extract tweet ID from the URL
Fetch tweet data from syndication.twitter.com/tweet-result?id={id} (or equivalent syndication endpoint)
Return JSON with: text, authorName, authorHandle, authorAvatarUrl, timestamp, imageUrls array
Return 400 for invalid URL format
Return 404 when tweet not found
Return 429 with retry message on rate limit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented using cdn.syndication.twimg.com endpoint. Returns proper JSON response with all required fields.

userStories.title: Fetch Full Thread via Syndication API
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to always fetch the full thread so I get complete context.
acceptanceCriteria: When fetching a tweet, also attempt to fetch the thread via syndication conversation endpoint
Thread tweets are ordered chronologically (oldest first)
Each tweet in thread includes: text, authorName, authorHandle, timestamp, imageUrls
If tweet is not part of a thread, return single tweet in an array
Response shape: { tweets: TweetData[] } where first tweet is earliest in thread
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Try syndication.twitter.com/timeline/conversation/{id} for thread data. If unavailable, fall back to single tweet only.

userStories.title: HTML Preview of Tweet
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a preview of the tweet before downloading so I can verify the content.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful fetch, hide the input form and show a preview section
Preview displays: author avatar, name, handle for each tweet
Tweet body text with preserved line breaks and links
Embedded images displayed inline
Timestamp shown below each tweet
For threads: each tweet rendered as a separate card in chronological order
Download PDF button appears below the preview
Convert Another button resets the page to the input form
Clean minimal layout that matches what the PDF will look like
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PDF Generation API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend endpoint that generates a PDF from tweet data.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/pdf endpoint accepts tweet data JSON (same shape as /api/tweet response)
Builds a custom HTML template from the tweet data (no Twitter widget JS)
HTML template includes: author section, tweet body, images, footer with original URL and generation timestamp
Uses Puppeteer to render the HTML to PDF (A4 page, print-friendly)
Returns PDF as application/pdf stream
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Download images and embed as base64 data URIs in the HTML template so Puppeteer can render them without network access.

userStories.title: PDF Download from Frontend
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click Download PDF and get the file saved to my computer.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Download PDF button sends tweet data to POST /api/pdf
Browser triggers file download with name format: handle-YYYY-MM-DD.pdf
Loading spinner shown on the button while PDF is generating
Button re-enables after download completes or errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Loading & Error States
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback during processing and when errors occur.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading spinner shown while fetching tweet data after clicking Generate PDF
Error message displayed for: invalid URL, tweet not found (404), protected tweet, network error, rate limit (429)
Error message appears inline below the input field
User can edit the URL and retry after an error without refreshing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
